sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) a flame, Son 8:6 comp. Chald., Psa 78:48. Targum.

(2) lightning, Psa 78:48. Hence poet., 76:4 , רִשְׁפֵי קֶשֶׁת “lightnings of the bow,” i.e. arrows, and Job 5:7, בְּנֵי רֶשֶׁף “sons of lightning,” i.e. ravenous birds flying with the rapidity of lightning, Job 5:7.

(3) a burning fever, a plague, by which the body is inflamed (compare חֵמָה heat and poison). Deu 32:24: לְחֻמֵי רֶשֶׁף “consumed with pestilence,” Hab 3:5 (where there is in the other hemistich דֶּבֶר plague).

(4) [Resheph], pr.n. 1Ch 7:25.
